result:
Done. The build succeeds with no warnings or errors.

I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, following the existing static-class style:

- **`EscapeField`** returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** is a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside kept as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. After each field, a comma means another field follows — so a trailing comma produces a final empty field, and the empty line parses to a single empty field (the loop body runs once before hitting end-of-line).

This satisfies the round-trip property `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` for all non-empty lists, and `NeedsQuoting` is untouched.
